ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
text: Ka neɛzaa wa soorɔ yɛ, yɛ deɛ yeli ŋaa, ‘A Daana la boɔrɔ a.’ A soba na bare a la ka yɛ taa wa ko ma wieouŋ lɛ.”
translated: When the man came and we saw him, we said, “The Daana has come.” He refused and said he had only come to greet us.

ref: Is this any way to show respect to your parents? You ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching.
text: A yɛ sããkommine yelwulii zuiŋ, yɛ vɛŋɛɛ la ka a Naaŋmen yelbiri ba taa tɔnɔ.
translated: They were playing in the evening when the chief's child came to call them.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and start back across the lake. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Tɔntɔbaalɛ, a Yeezu da eɛɛ la ka O potuuribo kpɛ a gbori a de weɛ ŋmaa a mane gaa a konkoori na kaŋ kyɛ ka O da bare a noba.
translated: Early in the morning, Jesus came to them walking on the sea, and when the disciples saw him walking on the water, they were terrified and said, “It is a ghost!”

ref: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
text: “Ka a Nensaala Bie wa wa a O veɛloŋ poɔ, a laŋ ne a malekere zaa, O pãã na wa zeŋ la O veɛloŋ nakogi zu.
translated: When a child is born and it is a boy, after eight days, they will name him and give him his name.

ref: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
text: Yɛ ta gbire, yɛ puoro, ka yɛ ta wa kpɛ enyaabo poɔ; a seɛ eŋ sereŋ boɔrɔ la, kyɛ ka a eŋɛ ba taa faŋ.”
translated: We came, we searched, and we entered the house of mourning; if it is not your turn, do not let it be your concern.

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: “Kyɛ a naa naŋ da wa kpɛ a die ka o nyɛ a saama, o da nyɛ la dɔɔ kaŋ a be ka o ba su a pɔgde tigri kparoo,
translated: If someone does something to you and you do the same to them, it is not good that you should go and burn their grass roof.

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Ba nembaaloŋ naŋ da kpɛ a Yeezu zuiŋ, O da de la O nu te seene ne a ba nimie. A daadaalɛ ka ba deɛ nyɛrɛ a tuuro a Yeezu gɛrɛ.
translated: They did not believe in Jesus, but he entrusted himself to the one who judges justly. They indeed pierced him in the side of Jesus.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: O naŋ da wa eŋ a noba sori baare, O da leɛ do la taŋ kaŋ zu ka O te puori.
translated: He went to the farm to work, but he came back without doing anything.

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: bonso, O da kanne ba la aŋa neɛ naŋ taa kpeɛ̃õ. O ne a Naaŋmen bege wulwulibo kannoo da ba yi taa.
translated: Good evening, it is not today that I have been coming. I am Naaŋmen's child who usually comes here.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Dobasugo da be la a be seŋ dire diibu.
translated: They are going to the farm to harvest yams.

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: bonso, kɔŋ da kpɛ ma la, ka yɛ ba ko ma bondirii, ka kɔŋnyuuri da kpɛ ma, ka yɛ ba ko ma koɔ̃ ka N nyu.
translated: Good evening, if hunger had not befallen me, I would not have gone to eat their food, and if thirst had not befallen me, I would not have gone to drink their water.

ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
text: Ba naŋ da wa ta a neŋyaga zie, dɔɔ kaŋ da wa la a Yeezu zie, a wa le gbi dumo O niŋesogɔ,
translated: They said that if you believe in him, you will not die but will have eternal life.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: Ka neɛzaa pãã wa yeli ko fo, ‘Nyɛ! a Mɛzaaya la kyɛ!’ Kyɛ bee O be la kyɛ! Yɛ ta sage de.
translated: If anyone at all tells you or says, 'No! the Mɛzaaya have gone!' They have gone or they have gone! Don't go after them.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli ko a dɔɔ nu kũũ, “Yi wa kyɛ.”
translated: And Jesus said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you.”
